name: General Sandbox
code:
-- Interactive Sandbox
local x: number = 10
local y: number = 20
print("Result: " .. tostring(x + y))

name: Challenge 1: Property Access
code:
-- PROPERTY ACCESS SIMULATION: 100,000 calculations per run
local drill = Instance.new("Part")
drill.Position = Vector3.new(0, 100, 0)

-- Scenario A: repeat the property lookup inside the hot loop
local startBad = os.clock()
local totalDepthA = 0
for i = 1, 100000 do
    totalDepthA += drill.Position.Y
end
local timeBad = (os.clock() - startBad) * 1000

-- Scenario B: cache once and read the local inside the hot loop
local startGood = os.clock()
local totalDepthB = 0
local cachedY = drill.Position.Y
for i = 1, 100000 do
    totalDepthB += cachedY
end
local timeGood = (os.clock() - startGood) * 1000

print(string.format("Repeated Property Access: %.4f ms\nCached Local Access: %.4f ms", timeBad, timeGood))
successMsg: Excellent job. In this browser-VM benchmark, caching drill.Position.Y avoided 100,000 repeated property lookups. Validate engine-facing gains separately in Roblox Studio.
failHint: Make sure you read drill.Position.Y exactly once before the loop, cache it in a local variable, and refer to that local variable inside the loop block.

name: Challenge 2: Closure Allocation Churn
code:
-- CLOSURE ALLOCATION CHURN SIMULATION: 1,000 batches of part updates
local parts = {}
for i = 1, 1000 do
    table.insert(parts, { Y = 0 })
end

-- Scenario A: create a capturing callback for every update
local startBad = os.clock()
for tick = 1, 1000 do
    for _, part in ipairs(parts) do
        -- This callback captures part, creating short-lived closure allocation pressure.
        task.spawn(function()
            part.Y += 1
        end)
    end
end
local timeBad = (os.clock() - startBad) * 1000

-- Scenario B: reuse one callback and pass each part as an argument
local startGood = os.clock()
local function updatePart(part)
    part.Y += 1
end

for tick = 1, 1000 do
    for _, part in ipairs(parts) do
        task.spawn(updatePart, part)
    end
end
local timeGood = (os.clock() - startGood) * 1000

print(string.format("Repeated Closure Creation: %.4f ms\nReused Function Reference: %.4f ms", timeBad, timeGood))
successMsg: In this browser-VM benchmark, reusing one callback reduced short-lived closure allocation. This demonstrates allocation churn, not a memory leak; profile the real workload separately in Roblox Studio.
failHint: Declare updatePart once outside the loops, then pass it and part separately to task.spawn.

name: Challenge 3: Table Rehashing
code:
-- 🏭 FACTORY DRILL TABLE PRE-ALLOCATION SIMULATION: 10,000 drill objects
local count = 10000

-- SCENARIO A: The Intermediate Developer (The Dynamic Reallocator)
local startBad = os.clock()
local drillsA = {}
for i = 1, count do
    -- Reallocating and rehashing memory dynamically as size increases
    drillsA[i] = { Y = 100, Power = i }
end
local timeBad = (os.clock() - startBad) * 1000

-- SCENARIO B: The Mastermind (The Pre-Allocation VM Optimizer)
local startGood = os.clock()

-- Pre-allocate memory block instantly here to prevent resizing overhead
local drillsB = table.create(count)
for i = 1, count do
    drillsB[i] = { Y = 100, Power = i }
end
local timeGood = (os.clock() - startGood) * 1000

print(string.format("❌ Unoptimized Table Rehash: %.4f ms\n✅ Optimized Pre-allocation: %.4f ms", timeBad, timeGood))
successMsg: Fantastic! By pre-allocating your array size using table.create(10000), you prevented the VM from triggering multiple resizing operations and re-indexing the array hash table.
failHint: Ensure you call table.create(count) to pre-allocate memory for drillsB before adding elements in the loop.

name: Challenge 4: Metatable Caching
code:
-- 🛠️ METATABLE INDEX CACHING SIMULATION: 50,000 index calls
local base = { Y = 100 }
local meta = {
    __index = function(t, key)
        -- Simulate costly validation on index
        local val = 0
        for i = 1, 10 do val += i end
        return base[key]
    end
}
local obj = setmetatable({}, meta)

-- SCENARIO A: The Intermediate Developer (Continuous __index Lookups)
local startBad = os.clock()
local sumA = 0
for i = 1, 50000 do
    sumA += obj.Y -- Triggers the metatable lookup function 50,000 times
end
local timeBad = (os.clock() - startBad) * 1000

-- SCENARIO B: The Mastermind (Cached Metatable Lookup)
local startGood = os.clock()
local sumB = 0
-- Cache the resolved index value locally to bypass __index entirely in loop
local cachedY = obj.Y
for i = 1, 50000 do
    sumB += cachedY
end
local timeGood = (os.clock() - startGood) * 1000

print(string.format("❌ Unoptimized Metatable Lookup: %.4f ms\n✅ Optimized Metatable Cached: %.4f ms", timeBad, timeGood))
successMsg: Splendid! By caching obj.Y locally, you avoided invoking the metatable's __index fallback method 50,000 times, avoiding lookup overhead.
failHint: Cache obj.Y in a local variable before starting Scenario B's loop block, and reference that local variable inside it.
